strengths: Three distinct, specific real-world objects are clearly identified with basic contextual information.
Each object is linked to the chosen IA prompt, demonstrating an awareness of different responses to new knowledge (acceptance, resistance, coercion).
Relevant images and background details show the objectsâ real-world existence beyond the classroom.
The exhibition structure addresses the prompt across multiple angles, highlighting variability in the impact of knowledge.
weaknesses: Explanations are often generalized or imprecise, with factual inaccuracies (e.g., overstating Darwinâs immediate acceptance, confusing Mauritania with Mauritius).
Analysis relies heavily on unsupported assertions rather than TOK concepts (e.g., ways of knowing, authority, evidence).
Justifications are repetitive and lack depth, with limited engagement with theoretical frameworks or sustained reasoning.
Citations are incomplete and the bibliography is skeletal, reducing traceability and academic rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work aligns with the descriptor for the 5â6 band: three objects and their contexts are clearly identified and linked to the IA prompt, and justifications are provided. However, imprecision, factual errors, reliance on assertion over analytical depth, and incomplete citations limit the quality. These factors place the exhibition at the lower end of the band, justifying a score of 5.

annotations.comment: Defining the new knowledge as the illegality of homosexuality conflates legal norms with epistemic knowledge. Clarify conceptual distinctions between legal information and knowledge in the TOK framework.
Identifying Article 308 of Mauritaniaâs penal code demonstrates the studentâs ambition to explore social and legal dimensions of how knowledge impacts beliefs in a realâworld context.
The bibliography entry âMennonite Church Articleâ is too vague. Include author, publication date, full URL, and access date to ensure traceability.
The citation âMauritania | Human Dignity Trustâ omits date and report details. Provide the full title, publication date, publisher, and URL for completeness.
Referring broadly to âthe homosexual communityâ as the knower oversimplifies diverse responses. Specify which group(s) and contexts are under consideration to refine the analysis.
The header omits the enactment date and source reference for the penal code article; including these details would clarify its context and strengthen object identification.
The student clearly states the selected prompt and frames the exhibitionâs focus on factors affecting changes in beliefs, setting a solid foundation for the objects to follow.

The detailed bibliographic description of the title page does not connect the image to the TOK prompt. Explicitly explain how the visual format of the original publication influenced the reception of new knowledge.

The introduction summarizes the prompt but omits TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, areas of knowledge) that could sharpen the conceptual framework and link each object explicitly to TOK methodology.

While identifying Darwinâs Origin of Species in education, the description of natural selection contains inaccuracies and conflates Wallaceâs role. Refining these historical details with authoritative sources will improve factual reliability.

The claim that Darwinâs theory was âaccepted rather easilyâ overlooks documented controversy. Incorporate specific historical examples (e.g., the WilberforceâHuxley debate) to nuance this assertion.
Asserting âirrefutable evidenceâ and swift acceptance lacks citation of primary or secondary historical sources. Include contemporary reactions or published critiques to support this account.

Repetitive reference to the scientist as a âlogical knower profileâ without engaging with specific TOK concepts (reason, emotion) or the natural sciences AOK weakens analytical depth. Connect this profile to established TOK terminology.

The discussion relies on general statements about upbringing and ingrained beliefs without empirical evidence or precise examples. Cite sociological or historical studies to substantiate these claims.
The student identifies a specific Mennonite doctrinal article and explains its religious context, demonstrating engagement with a communityâs established belief system as a realâworld object.
The webpage screenshot lacks an access date and full URL citation. Provide complete reference details to validate this objectâs authenticity and realâworld context.

The student rarely uses TOK-specific terms such as âways of knowingâ or âareas of knowledge,â which would deepen the conceptual analysis and explicitly frame each object within TOK methodology.
This commentary insightfully contrasts enduring religious beliefs with evolving scientific knowledge, offering a clear counterpoint that enriches the exhibitionâs narrative on belief change.
Interchanging âMauritian lawâ and âMauritaniaâ suggests confusion between the country and its legal system. Verify terminology and provide official source references for the penal code.
Describing penalties without citing an official legal text weakens credibility. Include an exact reference to the version and publication of the Mauritanian penal code.
The citation âNatural Selection: Charles Darwin & Alfred Russel Wallaceâ lacks publication details (publisher, year) and context for the link. Adopt a consistent referencing style.

Focusing solely on coercion overlooks the TOK distinction between coerced compliance and genuine belief revision. Discuss whether legal deterrence constitutes a change in values or mere behavior modification.
Including a bibliography demonstrates awareness of academic conventions and provides a foundation for tracing the sources used for each object.
The object title names the article but omits publication date or author details. Including such specifics in the title would strengthen the real-world precision.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
